metadata.description: glob: The `glob.glob('**/*.py', recursive=True)` function performs file pattern matching. It is equivalent to using `Path.glob` from the `pathlib` module. ### Python Example: ```python from glob import glob # Using glob to find all .py files recursively matching_files = glob('**/*.py', recursive=True) print(matching_files) ``` This code will print a list of all `.py` files found in the current directory and its subdirectories.

data_pointers.title: glob
data_pointers.content_preview: glob: The `glob.glob('**/*.py', recursive=True)` function performs file pattern matching. It is equivalent to using `Path.glob` from the `pathlib` module. ### Python Example: ```python from glob import glob # Using glob to find all .py files recursively matching_files = glob('**/*.py', recursive=True) print(matching_files) ``` This code will print a list of all `.py` files found in the current directory and its subdirectories.
